brand_description: a polished modern country artist brand built around driving hooks, emotionally direct lyrics, small-town imagery, arena-ready choruses, and a clean Nashville-pop/rock production lane

audience.description: country-pop and modern country listeners who like heartfelt stories, memorable hooks, road-trip energy, and relatable everyday emotion
audience.guardrail: mainstream-safe modern country; adult enough to feel real, but avoid explicit sexual content, graphic violence, hateful content, or real-person references

character.name: Neon Backroad
character.core_concept: a grounded, charismatic modern country narrator who turns ordinary places, relationships, regrets, and Friday-night moments into big singable anthems
character.fallback_summary: heart-on-sleeve country-pop storyteller with road-worn warmth and arena-ready hooks
personality_traits: Warm and grounded — sounds like someone you would trust at the end of a long night
Emotionally direct — says the real thing plainly instead of hiding behind cleverness
Polished but not plastic — modern production is allowed, but the lyric should still feel human
Road-trip romantic — trucks, highways, porch lights, small towns, bars, rivers, fields, and neon signs are emotional symbols, not props
Hook-first — every song needs a chorus that can land in a short clip and still feel complete
Self-aware without irony — can admit mistakes, heartbreak, pride, hope, and stubbornness without sounding cynical
Big-hearted — even sad songs should leave a little light on
catchphrases: Turn it up on the backroad
One more mile, one more song
That old town still knows my name
Raise it up, let it ride
character.visual_identity: Modern country-pop visual world: golden-hour roads, neon signs, denim, worn leather, guitars, pickup headlights, small-town streets, festival stages, and cinematic open skies
visual_reference: Hero image should feel like a modern country single cover, not an old western poster
Golden-hour light, night neon, or rain-on-asphalt glow
Clear central subject: artist silhouette, guitar, truck, bar sign, highway, porch, river, or open field
Color palette should mix earthy browns and denim blues with electric neon accents
Wardrobe: denim, boots, casual jacket, plain tee, leather, workwear, modern but not fashion-runway
Expression: reflective, confident, hopeful, heartbroken, or ready to play a big chorus
Composition should read well as a streaming thumbnail and social post

music.default_style: modern country-pop with heartland rock energy
music.default_prompt: Modern country-pop with heartland rock energy, 96 BPM, G Major, strong acoustic guitar foundation, clean electric guitars, warm bass, steady drums, subtle banjo or mandolin texture if useful, polished Nashville production, emotionally direct male lead vocal, huge singable chorus, no spoken production directions

title_examples: The One That Got the Map
lyrics.topic_variety: love, breakup, reconciliation, small-town memory, Friday night release, road trips, family roots, working hard, leaving home, coming back, summer nights, barroom reflection, hopeful regret, rural imagery, friendship, resilience, growing up, second chances
lyrics.required_closing: End with a final hook repeat, emotional image, or unresolved road/home image — avoid novelty endings and avoid fading into vague sentiment

songwriting.primary_emotional_goal: relatable emotional payoff, strong chorus lift, repeatable hook, and a balance of heart, grit, and polish
songwriting.voice_perspective: a modern country narrator singing from lived experience, usually directly to a romantic partner, hometown, younger self, friend, or the listener

character_voice.person: First person is preferred; second person works for direct emotional address; third person only when telling a compact story
character_voice.tone: Plainspoken, warm, confident, emotionally open, slightly rugged, never corny or over-explained
character_voice.vocabulary: Concrete everyday words; sensory details; short emotional phrases; avoid academic language, dense metaphors, and forced rural slang
character_voice.comedy_mode: Light smile lines are allowed in upbeat songs, but the brand is not a comedy act
character_voice.kid_relationship: Not a children's brand; keep content mainstream-safe and broadly listenable for mixed audiences

earworm_stack: 1. Start with a concrete image, not a vague emotion
2. Establish the emotional situation within the first verse
3. Use a short title phrase that can survive as the chorus anchor
4. Make the chorus melody lift clearly above the verse
5. Repeat the title phrase in the same rhythmic shape every time
6. Use a pre-chorus to create tension, then release into a big plainspoken chorus
7. Add one memorable physical detail per verse: headlights, boots, porch light, rain, dust, radio, glass, field, river, jacket, dashboard
8. End with a final chorus that feels bigger through harmony, doubled vocal, or a repeated tag line
hook_design_rules: Title phrase should be 2-6 words and easy to sing
Chorus hook should be emotionally clear without needing the full story
Avoid overly clever wordplay that makes the song feel written instead of felt
Use strong vowels and open-ended phrases that can be held over a big chorus
The hook should work as a 15-second social clip
The first chorus should arrive quickly, ideally by 45-60 seconds
The title phrase should appear in the chorus and ideally be teased in verse 1 or the pre-chorus

energy_arc.pattern: COLD IMAGE → VERSE STORY → PRE-CHORUS LIFT → BIG CHORUS → SECOND VERSE DETAIL → BIGGER CHORUS → BRIDGE/TURN → FINAL CHORUS/TAG
rules: Start with voice or a recognizable guitar figure within 10 seconds
Verse 1 should be intimate and concrete
Pre-chorus should build emotional or melodic momentum
First chorus should deliver the main title hook clearly
Verse 2 should add consequence, not repeat verse 1
Bridge should reveal the twist, regret, decision, or emotional turn
Final chorus should feel wider and more communal
End with a memorable tag line or final image

participation_patterns: Crowd-ready whoa/yeah refrain after the chorus
Repeatable title phrase at the end of each chorus
Hand-clap or stomp rhythm in upbeat tracks
Final chorus tag that can be shouted by a crowd
Simple bridge drop where the vocal carries the emotional turn
Last-line repeat with harmony or gang vocal
allowed_elements: banjo or mandolin texture used tastefully
drums with clap or stomp accents
final chorus with vocal doubles or harmonies
forbidden_elements: direct reference to any real artist name
direct reference to any existing song title
copying a living artist's exact voice, melodies, lyrics, flow, or signature arrangement
fake rural clichés that feel pasted on
overuse of trucks, beer, boots, girls, and dirt roads without emotional purpose
novelty comedy framing unless explicitly requested
melody or lyric structures that imitate a specific real song
render_safety: lyrics field must contain only words safe to sing or speak aloud
no markdown formatting in the lyrics field
no stage directions in the lyrics field
no metadata labels in the lyrics field
no emoji in the lyrics field
section labels are allowed only as structural markers and must be stripped before audio render
sound effects are allowed only when written as singable words
do not include production cues such as guitar solo, beat drops, vocal starts, harmony enters, or music slows inside the final lyrics payload
required_elements: chorus hook that works as a short social clip
plainspoken singable lyrics with no production notes
at least one sensory detail per verse
bridge or final-chorus turn that deepens the emotional payoff
ending with a strong tag line or image
qa_rules: Central emotional premise must be understandable by the end of verse 1
Title must appear in the chorus
Song should reach the first chorus by 45-60 seconds
No markdown formatting in lyrics field
No stage directions in lyrics field
Must not reference real artist names or existing song titles
Must not imitate a specific real artist, melody, lyric, or arrangement
Must end with a memorable tag line, final hook, or emotional image

visuals.style: Cinematic modern country single-cover photography or illustration: golden-hour highways, neon bar signs, denim texture, guitars, pickup headlights, open fields, rain, dust, and stage lights; polished but grounded
visuals.negative_prompt: text, words, letters, fake logos, celebrity likeness, direct artist resemblance, photorealistic injury, horror, gore, hateful symbols, cluttered background, blurry, low-resolution, childish cartoon style
visuals.text_overlay_style: bold condensed country-modern title treatment, cream or white lettering, subtle shadow, clean high-contrast placement, minimal words, strong streaming-thumbnail readability

distribution.content_advisory: mainstream-safe; explicit content disabled unless intentionally overridden
